videos.title: PinoyBikerNJ @ Gran Fondo National Series Highlands Butler NJ 2021
videos.description:
The hilly Highlands region outside of Butler features great climbs and ascends to the highest point in New Jersey. A great event for all-around cyclists who can power up short climbs and still have the energy to push the pace in a group.
 The Rides are 35, 60 & 100 miles up to 8000+ elevations. Check below link
https://www.granfondonationalseries.com/gran-fondo-highlands

Thanks for watching.

videos.title: Virtual Bike Ride | Highlands Gran Fondo 2025! | Butler, New Jersey | Indoor Cycling Workout Video
videos.description:
Cool Cycling Rides joins hundreds of cyclist for the 2025 Highlands Gran Fondo in Butler, New Jersey. Virtually ride from the start of the race to the first timed segment. Enjoy!

Please Like and Subscribe for more Cool Cycling Rides!

quotes.text: It was hot, hot, hot, scorching hot - this is the hottest day this year. It's definitely a challenging route very well organized, very safe so definitely one that you should try.
The views were crazy, the hills were stupid, but everything else was amazing.

You hit the first time section which climbs up Macca Pin only 1.5 miles into the course - on paper it sounds easy but you hit the highest grades which are north of 8 percent in the early part and there are lots of false flats you need to power through.

key_challenges.name: Macca Pin Climb
key_challenges.description: First time section hits just 1.5 miles into course - 4.7 miles with average 2.4% grade but steepest sections north of 8% in early part with lots of false flats to power through

key_challenges.name: Power Section
key_challenges.description: Third section of Macca Pin climb described as straightforward climbing with no false flats where riders go to their dark place, get out of saddle and mash it until the top

terrain_notes.text: Mix of flats and bumps in four timed segments with varying grades and false flats requiring switching between big and little ring throughout
Fourth section described as mini time trial with rolling terrain except for bump at end - can get into groove and hold consistent power

gear_mentions.text: Make sure equipment is ready morning of race and protect important gear with chamois cream for long days with lots of sweating during timed sections

race_day_tips.text: Make sure legs are warmed up since first time section climbs up Macca Pin only 1.5 miles into course
Know where time section starts and come in high - look for building on left and get speed up before the mark
If blocked during mass start group event, do mental reset and don't blow up legs - pace for the long game
Fuel up with plenty of coffee morning of race

additional_quotes.text: This is a mass start group event with people of varying capability. This is not a stage of the Giro. If you're blocked do a mental reset, don't blow up your legs, pace for the long game.

rider_intel.search_text: The Highlands Gran Fondo features a challenging mix of flats and bumps across four timed segments. The signature challenge is Macca Pin climb hitting just 1.5 miles into the 100-mile course - a 4.7 mile segment averaging 2.4% grade but with steepest sections over 8% early on and numerous false flats. Riders describe extreme heat as a major factor, calling it scorching hot. The course offers crazy views but stupid hills according to finishers. The event is well-organized with good safety measures, medical support, and helpful volunteers. As a mass start group event with varying rider capabilities, traffic management becomes important early on. The terrain requires frequent shifting between chainrings due to varying grades and rolling sections that feel like time trials with punchy bumps requiring momentum maintenance.
